title: Safety & security doc + dual-repo knowledge consolidation. A single authoritative, code-cited doc (corpus/ops/safety.md) on how the rosetta-extensions stack tooling stays safe: it never reads private/customer data (read-side: the tenant firewall AssertPlan/AssertCaptured, the public predicates byte-matched to firewall.go, the public-only data-DNA gene, read-only bounded capture) and it never touches production data or services (write-side: the 3-layer isolation guard CheckWrite/PreflightEnv/AssertClean, never-write shared Directus/prod-S3, the capture-source policy, the doubled n=0-dev guards, the audit-proven zero-pollution assertion). Cross-linked from all 4 sibling docs. Plus a dual-repo KB refresh: rosetta-extensions/knowledge/ to the v1.3 converged dev-equals-demo model + safety contract; root READMEs + corpus/ops/demo/ recipes to the unified stack-* skills + dev-as-peer.
is_last_milestone_note: M15 is the LAST milestone of v1.3 'stack party'. With it closed, all 4 milestones (M12->M15) are done and the release is ready for /developer-kit:close-release (merge release/01.30-stack-party -> main, tag v1.3). close-release is the user's separate next step, NOT run here.
kind: a documentation/consolidation milestone + harden drift-guard tests (the M14 shape). The deliverable is a claim surface (safety.md) trusted by future readers, plus drift guards that pin its load-bearing claims to code. No new production code path; the value is doc accuracy + reference integrity + the fail-closed docs<->code guards. All quoted predicates/symbols are byte-matched to the real extensions code.

tests.language: markdown (corpus/ops/safety.md + 4 siblings + READMEs + extensions knowledge/ — the deliverable) + go (extensions safety.md<->code drift guards) + python (dev-stack + stack-core/demo-stack/injection pytest, unchanged) + shellcheck/py_compile
tests.go_total_matcher_note: 713 counted with a consistent '^func Test' matcher across all 4 modules (alignment 43, clerkenstein 210, stack-seeding 236, stack-snapshot 224). The M14 headline (721) used a looser matcher; under the SAME '^func Test' matcher the M14-tag baseline is 706, so the milestone delta is the load-bearing figure: 706 -> 713 (+7), matching the 7 new drift-guard funcs.
tests.go_note: M15 added +7 Go test funcs, all docs<->code drift guards in the extensions clone: stack-snapshot/cmd/stacksnap/main_drift_test.go +3 (TestSafetyDocPredicatesMatchCode, TestSafetyDocNamesRealFirewallGates, TestSafetyDocBoundedReadSessionMatchesCode) and stack-seeding/isolation/safety_doc_drift_test.go +4 (TestSafetyDocClerkHostsMatchCode, TestSafetyDocDirectusTokenKeysMatchCode, TestSafetyDocForcedBucketOverride, TestSafetyDocNamesRealGuardSymbols). Each reads the real safety.md and asserts against actual code vars/symbols; each skips gracefully (t.Skip) when the sibling rosetta corpus is unreachable (per-stack pinned-tag clone / CI) so it guards drift only in the authoring copy where the doc is edited.

python_unchanged.note: M15 touched no Python production surface; 174 passed (dev-stack 38 + stack-core 54 + demo-stack 13 + stack-injection 69 — unchanged from M14).

tests.race: all 4 Go modules pass -race -count=1 (alignment, clerkenstein, stack-seeding 8 pkgs, stack-snapshot 12 pkgs all ok)
tests.gofmt_vet: gofmt -l empty + go vet clean on all 4 modules

tests.py_compile: all extension Python sources py_compile CLEAN
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean on both M15-touched packages — stack-snapshot/cmd/stacksnap 5x and stack-seeding/isolation 5x, each -count=1 -shuffle=on, all PASS. 0 flakes.

coverage.note: harden ran 4 passes (recorded in progress.md): 3 net-new drift-guard passes + 1 stabilization. M15 is a docs/consolidation milestone — the milestone-touched rosetta surface is entirely markdown (the new safety.md, the 4 sibling cross-links, READMEs + demo recipes, plan records). No code-coverage surface in rosetta. The code changes are the extensions-clone drift-guard tests + the M15-D4 fix.
coverage.drift_guard_completeness: every verbatim-literal / code-block / load-bearing-symbol claim in safety.md is now pinned by a fail-closed drift test: read-side public predicates (taxonomy + directus) + the AssertPlan/AssertCaptured gate names + the bounded-read-only SQL (source.DefaultBounds().SetupSQL); write-side the complete realClerkHosts + directusTokenKeys rejection lists + the forced STORAGE_S3_PUBLIC_BUCKET='' override + the CheckWrite/PreflightEnv/AssertClean guard symbols; plus the pre-existing --source-kinds + dropped-dump-flag guards. Remaining unpinned items are descriptive prose mentions (IsolationClass String values, AllowSharedOptIn, Record, Validate()) — deliberately not pinned (brittle prose string-matching).
coverage.mutation_proven: each guard proven fail-closed at close (Phase 2c): mutating safety.md's taxonomy predicate (organization_id IS NULL -> IS NOT NULL) tripped TestSafetyDocPredicatesMatchCode; renaming STORAGE_S3_PUBLIC_BUCKET tripped TestSafetyDocForcedBucketOverride. safety.md restored byte-identical after each; guards green again.

delivered.safety_md: corpus/ops/safety.md (net-new, 248 lines) — the authoritative read-side + write-side safety contract of the tooling. Dual-level: a PM 'two promises in one paragraph' section + an engineer deep-dive citing the real Go symbols/predicates/SQL. Part 1 (read side): the tenant firewall (AssertPlan + AssertCaptured), the per-surface public predicate (taxonomy organization_id IS NULL; directus private=false AND tenant_id IS NULL AND status='published'), the public-only data-DNA gene, the capture-source policy (dump-ingest -> bounded primary-read, MVCC, SET TRANSACTION READ ONLY + timeouts, no offline file reader). Part 2 (write side): the store registry (3 isolation classes), the 3-layer guard (CheckWrite/PreflightEnv/AssertClean), never-write shared Directus/prod-S3, the doubled n=0-dev guards, the audit-proven zero-pollution assertion. Plus a 'how this relates to the platform's own isolation' section + a clearly-labelled 'Future (v1.4 — DEF-M10-01)' forward pointer.
delivered.cross_links: back-links to safety.md added in all 4 siblings: db-access.md (read foundation), snapshot-spec.md (capture/replay + firewall, with a deep-link to safety.md §1.1), seeding-spec.md (write-side detail), architecture/security_compliance.md (the platform's own posture, the layer below). All resolve both directions.
delivered.extensions_kb_refresh: rosetta-extensions/knowledge/README.md refreshed to the v1.3 converged dev-equals-demo model (dev as a full-fidelity peer via dev-setdress.sh; the one generic stack-* operation set) + a cross-cutting safety-contract section (firewall gates + 3-layer guard named to real symbols, linking out to rosetta's safety.md). 0 stale pre-M14 skill names anywhere in the extensions knowledge/ base.
delivered.root_readme_recipe_refresh: root README.md + CLAUDE.md index safety.md (Key Documentation Locations); corpus/ops/demo/README.md (family index + 3 inline refs, incl the n=0 guard ref to safety.md §2.5) + recipe-snapshot-world.md refreshed for the unified stack-* skills + dev-as-peer + safety.md discovery.
delivered.m15_d4_fix: the M15-D4 Fate-1 fix landed in harden (ext c8a0589): the dev-setdress.sh:19-22 source comment AND the sibling provision-plan/main_test.go:179-181 comment both over-claimed 'stacksnap/stackseed independently refuse N=0 too' — stacksnap replay has NO N=0 guard (correct). Both corrected to match the shipped safety.md §2.5.

The consumption-clone path verified: guards t.Skip when the sibling corpus is unreachable, so a pinned-tag clone does not red. Conclusion: guards are genuinely fail-closed, not theatre. 0 bugs surfaced; no documented-risk acceptance needed.

notes: M15 is the LAST v1.3 milestone — closing it makes the release ready for /developer-kit:close-release. The defining shape: a claim-surface milestone. safety.md is a doc a future reader trusts to know the tooling is safe, so the bar is (a) every claim byte-matched to real code at author time (Phase 0b KB-fidelity GREEN) and (b) a fail-closed drift guard pinning each load-bearing claim so future code/doc drift becomes a test failure rather than a silent lie. The 7 new guards + the 2 mutation proofs at close are that bar met. The one close finding was a self-referential docs-accuracy drift (M15-D4's own decisions.md text lagged the harden fix) — fixed. Deferral re-audit GREEN, run release-wide (M12->M15) as the terminal-milestone pre-release sweep. Next: /developer-kit:close-release (merge release/01.30-stack-party -> main, tag v1.3) — the user's separate step.
